title: Enzymatic measurements for the detection of trypsin and carboxypeptidase A inhibitory activity
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"swtim","text":"This method can be used to measure the inhibitory activity of a biological extract against the target protease. In the associated Nature Protocol this method was used to determine the activity of C18 HPLC fractions before assaying non-convalent protein interactions by ‘intensity fading’ MALDI-TOF mass spectrometry.\r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":130,"length":15}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"gnhza","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5xrpj","text":"\r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"v1jnv","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"exo8c","text":"\r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6xbdv","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"0ruia","text":"It is advised that the inhibitory activity assay of each fraction should be performed in triplicate for each RP-HPLC fractionation to ensure reproducibility.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"wozof","text":"Trypsin gold, MS grade (Promega, cat. no. V5280): 100 μg lyophilized powder\r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fiftp","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"lbw18","text":"Synthetic trypsin substrate BAEE Prepare aliquots of 1.06 mg ml-1 BAEE in 0.05 M Tris buffer, pH 8.0.\r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":63,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"csnms","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"p2uoj","text":"CRITICAL Store at -20 °C to avoid degradation.\r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"jm5xa","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dkgbb","text":"\r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"smgnl","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"wcomf","text":"Bovine carboxypeptidase A (Sigma-Aldrich, cat. no. 21940): approximately 60 units per mg protein (approximately 20 mg ml-1); alternatively, recombinant human carboxypeptidase A (hCPA) can be used\r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":120,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ykym8","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"kvi8v","text":"Synthetic carboxypeptidase substrate: N-(4-methoxyphenylazoformyl)-Phe-OH-potassium salt (Bachem AG, cat. no. M-2245)  Prepare aliquots of 3.65 mg ml-1 in 2% DMSO.\r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"xeqjl","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ak1ho","text":"CRITICAL Store at -20 °C to avoid degradation.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Incubate in a quartz cell 10 μl of biological sample (from each re-dissolved chromatographic fraction) with 50 μl trypsin (33 μg ml -1 in 20 mM CaCl 2 , 0.1 M Tris, pH 8.0) and 790 μl activity buffer (20 mM CaCl 2 , 0.1 M Tris, pH 8.0) for 3 min at room temperature.
Repeat Step 1 in another quartz cell and add 10 μl activity buffer (20 mM CaCl 2 , 0.1 M Tris, pH 8.0) instead of biological sample in order to obtain the control sample.
Add 150 μl of the substrate N-benzoyl-arginine ethyl ester hydrochloride to each quartz cell (see REAGENTS).
Gently mix all the quartz cells and measure the absorbance change at 254 nm and 25 °C over 2 min, producing 240 time points (signal averaging time = 0.5 s).
Control slopes should be 0.050 ± 0.003 units of absorbance (U.Abs.) per min.
Biological samples yielding slopes below 0.035 U.Abs. per min are taken for intensity fading MS assays.

Incubate in a quartz cell 10 μl biological sample (from each re-dissolved chromatographic fraction) with 2 μl carboxypeptidase A (35 μg ml -1 in 20 mM Tri-HCl buffer, 0.5 M NaCl, pH 7.5) and 978 μl activity buffer (20 mM Tri-HCl buffer, 0.5 M NaCl, pH 7.5) for 3 min at room temperature.
Repeat Step 1 in another quartz cell and add 10 μl activity buffer (50 mM Tri-HCl buffer, 100 mM NaCl, pH 7.5) instead of biological sample in order to obtain the control sample.
Add 10 μl of the substrate N-(4-methoxyphenylazoformyl)-Phe-OH to each quartz cell (see REAGENTS).
Gently mix all the quartz cells and measure the absorbance change at 350 nm and 25 °C over 3 min, producing 360 time points (signal averaging time = 0.5 s).
Control slopes should be 0.12 ± 0.02 U.Abs. per min.
Biological samples yielding slopes below 0.060 U.Abs. per min are taken for 'intensity fading MS' assays.

Detection of non-covalent protein interactions by 'intensity fading' MALDI-TOF mass spectrometry: applications to proteases and protease inhibitors by Oscar Yanes, Josep Villanueva, Enrique Querol & Francesc X Aviles published in Nature on 04 Sep, 2007 https://dx.doi.org/10.1038/nprot.2006.487
creator: Protocols Importer
